text.title: img-gen-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for img-gen-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Expecting DALL-E 3 to render precise text within the image.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: All current image generation models including DALL-E 3 are unreliable for rendering text strings inside images — characters are frequently garbled, misspelled, or completely wrong. Do not depend on this for production images.

failure-mode.description: Running ThreadPoolExecutor with 10 threads on a DALL-E tier-1 API key.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DALL-E 3 rate limit is per-organization (5 img/min), not per-key. 10 concurrent threads will immediately exhaust the shared limit and all fail with 429. Serialize with delay instead.

failure-mode.description: Caching DALL-E provider URLs and resuming a pipeline hours later.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DALL-E pre-signed URLs expire in ~60 minutes. Late-stage resume attempts get 404 on all cached URLs. Download immediately and store locally/in blob storage.

failure-mode.description: Passing JPEG to DALL-E 2 variation or edit endpoints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DALL-E 2 variation/edit endpoints require PNG images with an alpha channel. JPEG causes a cryptic 400 error. Convert to PNG first. (Better: use DALL-E 3 or Flux instead — DALL-E 2 endpoints are deprecated.)

failure-mode.description: Assuming reimagine_image output matches the original image.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The describe-then-generate cycle produces a loosely-related new image, not a variation. It is creative and often useful, but stakeholders expecting similarity will be disappointed. Set expectations upfront.
